environment.note: leg 7 of the distiller saga — wired-limit hypothesis (PLAN-V2 §5); limit raised by rc 2026-06-12, reverts on reboot; mlx-lm 0.31.3 (confirmed latest)

resume_history.note: leg7 PRE-REGISTERED (EXPERIMENT-LOG 2026-06-12): predicted ~55/45 pass at 13.0GB wired; correction vs pre-registration — clear-cache-threshold was ALREADY 1GB in legs 4-6 (state.json hparams), so the only new levers vs leg 6 are the wired limit and the 2B→4B model swap.

leg7 RESOLVED the memory question: 240+ iters, 3 full val passes, peak 5.45GB, ZERO OOM under wired_limit 13312 — where legs 1-6 (2B) died before iter 1. But loss diverged to NaN between iter 40 (0.891, healthy) and 60 at lr 1e-4 on long compression records; all checkpoints post-NaN, discarded. leg8 = fresh start, lr halved to 5e-5 (the dogfood/level2 lesson), single factor changed.

leg8 NaN at the SAME window (iter 41-59) at lr 5e-5 — LR acquitted, data indicted. Root cause found mechanically (tokenizer scan, 2 min): records whose templated prompt >= max-seq-length leave zero trainable completion tokens under --mask-prompt -> masked-loss division by zero. 5 such in the 365-train, 1 in the 44-valid at cap 2048. leg9 = full-fidelity rebuild (rc-approved): original 546/68/68 split reconstructed via deterministic dedupe.py (683->682) + split_data.py --seed 42, PROVEN by test-id identity + zero-leak checks; filtered at >=16 trainable tokens under cap 3072 -> train 536 (10 dropped, ids in log) / valid 68 / test 68 untouched. No truncation remains (longest kept record fits). lr stays 5e-5.
